name: PMNS mixing-pattern puzzle (lepton anarchy vs CKM hierarchy)
summary: The lepton mixing matrix (PMNS) has two large mixing angles (theta_12 ~ 33deg, theta_23 ~ 49deg) and a sizeable theta_13 ~ 8.6deg, in stark contrast with the small-and-hierarchical CKM mixing in the quark sector (lambda ~ 0.22). The qualitative difference between quark and lepton mixing demands a distinct flavor mechanism for leptons; tribimaximal patterns and modular flavor symmetries try to capture the structure but the physical origin of large lepton mixing is unsettled.

characteristic_numbers.note: Solar mixing angle, much larger than the analogous CKM Cabibbo angle.
Daya Bay 2012 / RENO 2012 confirmed theta_13 sizable, killed exact tribimaximal.
Quark-lepton complementarity: the sum of Cabibbo angle and solar mixing is suspiciously close to pi/4. May be coincidence or hint at GUT-scale relation.

principles_stressed.note: Why the qualitative difference between quark and lepton mixing? Possibilities: (a) anarchic Yukawas in the neutrino sector + Majorana mass (Hall-Murayama-Weiner 2000, 'anarchy'); (b) discrete flavor symmetries (A4, S4, modular) producing tribimaximal-like patterns at leading order; (c) GUT-scale quark-lepton complementarity. None is established.

history: Quark mixing measured in the 1970s-80s; lepton mixing established with Super-Kamiokande (atmospheric, 1998) and SNO (solar, 2001). theta_13 measured to be sizable by Daya Bay (2012, arXiv:1203.1669) and RENO (2012, arXiv:1204.0626). Anarchy proposal: Hall, Murayama, Weiner (2000), 'Neutrino mass anarchy', Phys. Rev. Lett. 84, 2572, arXiv:hep-ph/9911341. Quark-lepton complementarity: Smirnov (2004, arXiv:hep-ph/0402264).

references.citation: Daya Bay Collaboration, 'Observation of electron-antineutrino disappearance at Daya Bay', Phys. Rev. Lett. 108, 171803 (2012)
Hall, Murayama, Weiner, 'Neutrino mass anarchy', Phys. Rev. Lett. 84, 2572 (2000)
